title: Glistening Dehydrated Malaysian Noodles Bowl
description: Umami, silky, and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly, this meal-prep dish combines the flavors of Malaysia with the convenience of dehydrated noodles.
instructions: Soak the red rice noodles in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
In a blender or food processor, combine the vegetable broth, coconut oil, ginger, Thai sweet basil, horapa, tamari, and maple syrup. Blend until smooth.
In a large skillet, heat the blended mixture over medium heat. Add the kale and shiitake mushrooms. Cook until the kale is wilted and the mushrooms are tender.
Add the cooked noodle mixture to the skillet and stir to combine. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a wooden board or serving platter.
Dehydrate the noodle mixture in a food dehydrator at 135°F (57°C) for 1-2 hours, or until dry and slightly flexible.
Break the dehydrated noodles into smaller pieces and serve in individual bowls.
Garnish with additional Thai sweet basil and horapa, if desired.
